4 INTRODUCTION |

of Contract; (6) Breach of Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing; (7) Intentional

3 GENERAL ALLEGATIONS
11. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that the principal office and |
6 || headquarters for all defendants is in San Francisco, California, San Francisco County.
7 12. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that she began her
employment with USF in May 1995. Over the course of WESTERFIELD’s seven years of |

10 employment with defendants, she experienced a steady succession of promotions. :

11 || WESTERFIELD began as a reader for students with visual disabilities. However, little more |

12 || than a year later, Plaintiff was perceived to be doing so well that she was promoted to designing

13 and coordinating educational accommodations for those same disabled students as well as

. tutoring and teaching disabled students to use adaptive equipment. In June 1999, Plaintiff was

16 || Promoted yet again to and Instructional Designer and Coordinator of Academic

17 || Accommodations.

18 13. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that defendants began to

, humiliate and verbally abuse WESTERFIELD after she was promoted to Instructional Designer |

1 and Coordinator of Academic Accommodations and in June 1999 outside the scope of her

92 ||employment. Defendant BOHN, a disabled woman in her mid-twenties, yelled at plaintiff and |

23 || ordered her to perform menial tasks, which were not part of her job description. BOHN also :

24 refused to communicate with WESTERFIELD by ignoring her and refusing to respond to !

~ communications attempted by Plaintiff. |

7 14. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that defendants made |

28 || repeated derogatory age related comments to WESTERFIELD regarding menopause and not |
THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT 4 CASE NO. CGC-03-417577 |

 
|
1 || being able to think as well as younger people. Defendants made additional derogatory age |
2 |\ related comments as to old timers, battleaxes and older people not thinking as fast. |
3 15. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that defendant BOHN
forced WESTERFIELD to perform her job responsibilities. BOHN also had the audacity to take |
6 || credit for the work that she forced WESTERFIELD to complete. BOHN also yelled at Plaintiff |
7 || and humiliated her in front of others. Plaintiff was intentionally excluded from meetings and a |
8 || work related conference and excluded from access to information needed in order to fulfill her
: job responsibilities in contrast to her co-workers. Defendants also failed to acknowledge
1 WESTERFIELD’s birthday, also, in contrast to her co-workers. .
12 16. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that WESTERFIELD
13 || attempted to work around the egregious conduct of BOHN. However, BOHN’s outrageous |
14 behavior became unbearable. Plaintiff complained to defendant DAVIS in April 2001. ;
: However, DAVIS completely failed to take steps in order to correct BOHN’s wrongful conduct
7 against WESTERFIELD.
18 17. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that subsequent to
19 || WESTERFIELD’s complaints, BOHN failed to either have or complete meetings which |
20 addressed Plaintiff's performance evaluation, which included several unfounded negative |
i. comments by BOHN. Despite BOHN’s attempt to sabotage WESTERFIELD at USF, Plaintiff
73 || received a merit pay increase, but it served to only cause BOHN’s hostility to escalate. BOHN’s |
24 || aggression towards WESTERFIELD was witnessed by others. |
25 18. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that WESTERFIELD |
26 discovered in February 2000 that defendant USF was not in compliance with the directives of the
Americans with Disabilities Act (hereafter “ADA”) as to many different types of |
|

|
|

 
1 || accommodations. The sub-standard accommodations concerned copiers, computers and tables. |
2 || Disabled students at USF were being deprived access to equipment that they needed to fulfill |
3 their educational objectives despite the fact that USF was received government funding. |
19. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that she received a |
6 || Satisfactory evaluation for her work performance at USF in May 2000. WESTERFIELD also |
7 || became aware that defendants did not have a required “504 transition plan” pursuant to ADA |
8 directives. Plaintiff continued to be actively involved in attempting to bring defendants into
- compliance with the ADA. WESTERFIELD notified BOHN and others of the ADA compliance
11 violations, but defendants’ hostility towards her only increased. WESTERFIELD’s informing
12 || defendants of their ADA non-compliance culminated with negative comments on her May 2001
13 || performance evaluation. It was defendants’ policy to allow its employees adequate opportunities
14 to review and make comments. However, Plaintiff was barely given one hour to review and
. sign. BOHN also cancelled and was late to multiple meetings that were scheduled by Plaintiff |
17 regarding her evaluation.
18 20. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that she assumed that |
19 || defendants wanted to comply with the law and, thus, continued to attempt to work with them to |
20 bring USF into ADA compliance. However, defendants only branded her as a trouble maker and
: . terminated her. Defendant JOHNSON participated in a hoax to remove WESTERFIELD from |
i 3 || her position by pretending that her job was being eliminated because student numbers, needs and
24 || accommodations had changed.
25 21. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that WESTERFIELD was
| . given a letter of termination in February 2002. The letter stated that her position was being :
terminated because her department was being reorganized in order to better meet increased

1 || student demand. When Plaintiff's position was terminated, the program and funding remained :

22. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that WESTERFIELD was |
6 || given letters of recommendation by defendant JOHNSON and DAVIS and told that she could
7 || apply for another position within the university. JOHNSON told others that Plaintiff would be |

1 {22 Assistant Coordinator of Disability Services position in April 2002. That position was |

13 || interview.

: the position to two women under the age of 40. Plaintiff notified defendants in writing of her

20 woman’s qualifications were inferior to those of Plaintiff. |
i. 24. Plaintiff is informed and believes and thereon alleges that she entered into a |

*6 she would not be an at will employee. Defendants further indicated that WESTERFIELD would
. have employment forever - as long as USF had disabled students. |

1 and conditions of plaintiffs' employment. |
